analysis_notes: Loaded dataset.parquet locally. Screened all feature_* columns against pfs_months, fit a full main-effects OLS model with all feature_* columns, scanned all pairwise interactions with constituent main effects, and refit selected interactions adjusted for all feature_* main effects.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher `feature_041` values are associated with higher `pfs_months` after adjusting for all other `feature_*` columns.

analyses.code: OLS: pfs_months ~ all feature_* main effects; coefficient for feature_041.
analyses.result_summary: In the full main-effects OLS model, `feature_041` had an adjusted coefficient of +0.17584 months of `pfs_months` per one-unit increase (SE=0.00039, p<1e-300).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher `feature_025` values are associated with lower `pfs_months` after adjusting for all other `feature_*` columns.

analyses.code: OLS: pfs_months ~ all feature_* main effects; coefficient for feature_025.
analyses.result_summary: In the full main-effects OLS model, `feature_025` had an adjusted coefficient of -1.17140 months per one-unit increase (SE=0.00570, p<1e-300).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `feature_036=1` have lower `pfs_months` than patients with `feature_036=0` after adjusting for all other `feature_*` columns.

analyses.code: OLS: pfs_months ~ all feature_* main effects; coefficient for feature_036.
analyses.result_summary: `feature_036=1` was associated with -1.36149 months of `pfs_months` relative to `feature_036=0` in the full main-effects model (SE=0.00776, p<1e-300).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `feature_043=1` have higher `pfs_months` than patients with `feature_043=0` after adjusting for all other `feature_*` columns.

analyses.code: OLS: pfs_months ~ all feature_* main effects; coefficient for feature_043.
analyses.result_summary: `feature_043=1` was associated with +0.94295 months of `pfs_months` relative to `feature_043=0` in the full main-effects model (SE=0.00964, p<1e-300).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher `feature_056` values are associated with lower `pfs_months` after adjusting for all other `feature_*` columns.

analyses.code: OLS: pfs_months ~ all feature_* main effects; coefficient for feature_056.
analyses.result_summary: `feature_056` had an adjusted coefficient of -0.07606 months of `pfs_months` per one-unit increase (SE=0.00100, p<1e-300).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher `feature_050` values are associated with higher `pfs_months` after adjusting for all other `feature_*` columns.

analyses.code: OLS: pfs_months ~ all feature_* main effects; coefficient for feature_050.
analyses.result_summary: `feature_050` had an adjusted coefficient of +0.46940 months of `pfs_months` per one-unit increase (SE=0.00770, p<1e-300).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `feature_001=1` have lower `pfs_months` than patients with `feature_001=0` after adjusting for all other `feature_*` columns.

analyses.code: OLS: pfs_months ~ all feature_* main effects; coefficient for feature_001.
analyses.result_summary: `feature_001=1` was associated with -0.35437 months of `pfs_months` relative to `feature_001=0` in the full main-effects model (SE=0.00807, p<1e-300).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `feature_015=1` have lower `pfs_months` than patients with `feature_015=0` after adjusting for all other `feature_*` columns.

analyses.code: OLS: pfs_months ~ all feature_* main effects; coefficient for feature_015.
analyses.result_summary: `feature_015=1` was associated with -0.28655 months of `pfs_months` relative to `feature_015=0` in the full main-effects model (SE=0.00808, p=1.126e-275).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher `feature_029` values are associated with lower `pfs_months` after adjusting for all other `feature_*` columns.

analyses.code: OLS: pfs_months ~ all feature_* main effects; coefficient for feature_029.
analyses.result_summary: `feature_029` had an adjusted coefficient of -0.00501 months of `pfs_months` per one-unit increase (SE=0.00024, p=6.843e-98).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `feature_066=1` have lower `pfs_months` than patients with `feature_066=0` after adjusting for all other `feature_*` columns.

analyses.code: OLS: pfs_months ~ all feature_* main effects; coefficient for feature_066.
analyses.result_summary: `feature_066=1` was associated with -0.35535 months of `pfs_months` relative to `feature_066=0` in the full main-effects model (SE=0.01890, p=7.312e-79).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher `feature_028` values are associated with lower `pfs_months` after adjusting for all other `feature_*` columns.

analyses.code: OLS: pfs_months ~ all feature_* main effects; coefficient for feature_028.
analyses.result_summary: `feature_028` had a small adjusted negative coefficient of -0.000425 months of `pfs_months` per one-unit increase (SE=0.000048, p=5.544e-19).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `feature_042=1` have higher adjusted `pfs_months` than patients with `feature_042=0` after controlling for all other `feature_*` columns.

analyses.code: OLS: pfs_months ~ all feature_* main effects; coefficient for feature_042.
analyses.result_summary: `feature_042=1` had a small positive adjusted coefficient of +0.02138 months, but this was not statistically significant (SE=0.02289, p=0.3503). The marginal signal did not persist after full adjustment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive association of `feature_043=1` with `pfs_months` is smaller among patients with `feature_001=1` than among patients with `feature_001=0`.

analyses.code: OLS: pfs_months ~ all feature_* main effects + feature_001:feature_043 interaction.
analyses.result_summary: The fully adjusted `feature_001:feature_043` interaction was -1.63231 months (SE=0.01812, p<1e-300). Unadjusted subgroup means were 5.773 months for feature_001=0/feature_043=1 vs 4.117 for 0/0, but 4.143 for 1/1 vs 4.116 for 1/0.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive association of `feature_043=1` with `pfs_months` is smaller among patients with `feature_015=1` than among patients with `feature_015=0`.

analyses.code: OLS: pfs_months ~ all feature_* main effects + feature_015:feature_043 interaction.
analyses.result_summary: The fully adjusted `feature_015:feature_043` interaction was -1.46275 months (SE=0.01912, p<1e-300). Unadjusted subgroup means showed a large feature_043-associated increase when feature_015=0, but almost none when feature_015=1.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive association of `feature_043=1` with `pfs_months` decreases as `feature_029` increases.

analyses.code: OLS: pfs_months ~ all feature_* main effects + feature_029:feature_043 interaction.
analyses.result_summary: The fully adjusted `feature_029:feature_043` interaction was -0.02580 months per one-unit higher `feature_029` among `feature_043=1` patients (SE=0.00059, p<1e-300).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The joint association of `feature_015=1` and `feature_001=1` with `pfs_months` is less negative than expected from adding their separate main effects.

analyses.code: OLS: pfs_months ~ all feature_* main effects + feature_015:feature_001 interaction.
analyses.result_summary: The fully adjusted `feature_015:feature_001` interaction was +0.51254 months (SE=0.01621, p=1.757e-219), supporting a sub-additive joint adverse association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive association of `feature_043=1` with `pfs_months` is smaller among patients with `feature_066=1` than among patients with `feature_066=0`.

analyses.code: OLS: pfs_months ~ all feature_* main effects + feature_066:feature_043 interaction.
analyses.result_summary: The fully adjusted `feature_066:feature_043` interaction was -0.98816 months (SE=0.04563, p=5.188e-104). In unadjusted means, feature_043 was favorable when feature_066=0 but not when feature_066=1.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive association between `feature_041` and `pfs_months` is weaker at higher levels of `feature_025`.

analyses.code: OLS: pfs_months ~ all feature_* main effects + feature_041:feature_025 interaction.
analyses.result_summary: The fully adjusted `feature_041:feature_025` interaction was -0.00732 months per one-unit increase in both predictors (SE=0.00057, p=7.704e-38), indicating attenuation of the feature_041 slope as feature_025 rises.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with both `feature_042=1` and `feature_043=1` have higher `pfs_months` than expected from adding the separate associations of `feature_042` and `feature_043`.

analyses.code: OLS: pfs_months ~ all feature_* main effects + feature_042:feature_043 interaction.
analyses.result_summary: The fully adjusted `feature_042:feature_043` interaction was +0.88849 months (SE=0.05553, p=1.296e-57). This indicates that the otherwise weak feature_042 signal is concentrated in the feature_043=1 subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive association between `feature_041` and `pfs_months` is weaker among patients with `feature_036=1` than among patients with `feature_036=0`.

analyses.code: OLS: pfs_months ~ all feature_* main effects + feature_041:feature_036 interaction.
analyses.result_summary: The fully adjusted `feature_041:feature_036` interaction was -0.00693 months per one-unit higher `feature_041` among `feature_036=1` patients (SE=0.00078, p=5.893e-19).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative association between `feature_029` and `pfs_months` is weaker among patients with `feature_001=1` than among patients with `feature_001=0`.

analyses.code: OLS: pfs_months ~ all feature_* main effects + feature_001:feature_029 interaction.
analyses.result_summary: The fully adjusted `feature_001:feature_029` interaction was +0.00802 months per one-unit higher `feature_029` among `feature_001=1` patients (SE=0.00048, p=4.984e-62), showing attenuation of the negative feature_029 slope.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative association between `feature_029` and `pfs_months` is weaker among patients with `feature_015=1` than among patients with `feature_015=0`.

analyses.code: OLS: pfs_months ~ all feature_* main effects + feature_015:feature_029 interaction.
analyses.result_summary: The fully adjusted `feature_015:feature_029` interaction was +0.00773 months per one-unit higher `feature_029` among `feature_015=1` patients (SE=0.00050, p=3.969e-54), again indicating attenuation of the feature_029 adverse slope.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among patients with `feature_060=1`, the association between `feature_064` and `pfs_months` is more negative than among patients with `feature_060=0` after full adjustment.

analyses.code: OLS: pfs_months ~ all feature_* main effects + feature_064:feature_060 interaction.
analyses.result_summary: The pairwise screen suggested a negative `feature_064:feature_060` interaction, but after adjusting for all feature main effects the coefficient was -0.03813 months and was not significant (SE=0.02046, p=0.0624).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The joint association of higher `feature_025` and `feature_036=1` with `pfs_months` is less negative than expected from adding their separate adverse main effects.

analyses.code: OLS: pfs_months ~ all feature_* main effects + feature_025:feature_036 interaction.
analyses.result_summary: The fully adjusted `feature_025:feature_036` interaction was +0.04419 months (SE=0.01145, p=0.000113), a small but significant sub-additive interaction between two adverse predictors.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with both `feature_023=1` and `feature_068=1` have higher `pfs_months` than expected from adding the separate associations of `feature_023` and `feature_068`.

analyses.code: OLS: pfs_months ~ all feature_* main effects + feature_023:feature_068 interaction.
analyses.result_summary: The fully adjusted `feature_023:feature_068` interaction was +0.34324 months (SE=0.14930, p=0.0215). This was directionally positive but much weaker than the dominant feature_043-related interactions.
